lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: You will need to feed King Awowogei his favourite dish.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's that then?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: I have to admit I'm not sure, maybe you should ask him?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ask the version of him on Ape Atoll?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Yes, of course.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can we talk about what happened in the dining room?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Wow! You were incredible! The way you defeated the Culinaromancer, you made it seem so easy!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... I don't suppose you can remember how I defeated him can you?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Of course! Which bit can't you remember doing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The monkey king... However did I save him from the culinaromancer...?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Well, it was something that sounded ABSOLUTELY DISGUSTING that you picked up on Ape Atoll...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yeah... I remember now...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Did you want a reminder on any other of your brave exploits?

lines.speaker: King Awowogei
lines.text: Hello stranger, what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have a particular favourite dish?

lines.speaker: King Awowogei
lines.text: What? No. Definitely not.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure?...

lines.speaker: King Awowogei
lines.text: Absolutely, I can't let you have sensitive information like that...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you do have a favourite dish, but you don't want to tell me.

lines.speaker: King Awowogei
lines.text: I won't tell you anything - and neither will the three monkeys, I made sure of that. I can't have any monkey knowing about it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So if I talk with these three monkeys they'll tell me all about it?

lines.speaker: King Awowogei
lines.text: NO! Now go away and leave me in peace.

lines.speaker: King Awowogei
lines.text: Go away, I have nothing further to say to you!

sections.title: Talking to the three wise monkeys

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: What? eh? Did you say something?

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Who's there? Hello?

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: mmmmghmhhmh

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Is there somebody there?

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: mmmmghhh mhhhh

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's something familiar about you guys...

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: I'm Mizaru - This is Iwazaru [sic, no fullstop]

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: mmhggmmmhh

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: He says hello, and this is Kikazaru [sic, no fullstop]]

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Oi, you, pay attention!

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: What? Oh, Hello who are you? I'm Kikazaru.

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: I've already said that.

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: What? Oh, This is Mizaru and Kikazaru.

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: I'VE ALREADY SAID THAT.

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Not very bright that one.

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: ggmmhhmm

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: (sarcastically) Yeah, while you're the clever monkey around here aren't you?

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (Iwazaru waves his arms again. Whatever he said it wasn't very polite.)

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: Hee Hee Hee, you're so rude.

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Grrr. Do you have any other questions?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So What exactly are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Oh, just monkeying around.

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (Iwazaru slaps his forehead)

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: You look trustworthy, I'll tell you a secret.

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: (whispers) We're on a top secret mission, given to us by the King...

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: We are the exalted Guardians of one of the King's biggest secrets.

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Can't say any more than that or the King would go Ape on us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh go on...

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: No. We will protect the recipe with our lives...

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: grrmmh fffhmmm (Iwazaru looks disgruntled)

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: Yeah, I agree with Iwazaru. The king CURSED us!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did he curse you?

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: hmhmhhhh mmgghh

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: I know, I know, I'll tell him

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: NO!, I forbid you to tell this stranger anything!

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: I can't hear you...

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Yeah, well hear this gnome-brain! (He slaps Kikazaru)

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: Ow! There was this recipe right.

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Stop it!

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: (Oof!) He liked it so much he married the cook! YEAH? Have some of this! (poke!)

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: AARGH not in the eye! (kick)

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: (Oof!) The King thought it was magic and AAAIEEE! He got a priest to curse us to keep quiet. (headbutt!)

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: Ha! Serves you right! Anyway, The priest only had enough ingredients for one curse, so when he tried to keep us all from telling anyone the recipe, each of us only got part of the curse. That's why I can't hear

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: anything now.

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: mmmmghhh (he nods his head)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me what this recipe is then?

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: How do you know about that - It's top secret information you know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I am also on a top secret mission.

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Ah I see, can you tell me what the mission is?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, it's on a strict need to know basis. I'm sure you understand.

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Of course, of course.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: However, I think I can trust you with sensitive material.

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (Iwazaru raises an eyebrow dubiously. You give him a wink.)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: (You whisper in Mizaru's ear) I've been charged with organising a secret party to celebrate the king's birthday, keep it quiet though, don't let a single monkey know.

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Well, in that case, you definitely do need to know the recipe.

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: hmhmhhhh mmgghh

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: I know, I know, I'll tell him

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: NO!, I forbid you to tell this stranger anything!

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: I can't hear you...

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Yeah, well hear this gnome-brain! (He slaps Kikazaru)

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: Ow! There was this recipe right.

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Stop it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So What exactly are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Oh, just monkeying around.

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (Iwazaru slaps his forehead)

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: You look trustworthy, I'll tell you a secret.

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: (whispers) We're on a top secret mission, given to us by the King...

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: We are the exalted Guardians of one of the King's biggest secrets.

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Can't say any more than that or the King would go Ape on us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh go on...

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: No. We will protect the recipe with our lives...

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: grrmmh fffhmmm (Iwazaru looks disgruntled)

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: Yeah, I agree with Iwazaru. The king CURSED us!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did he curse you?

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: hmhmhhhh mmgghh

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: I know, I know, I'll tell him

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: NO!, I forbid you to tell this stranger anything!

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: I can't hear you...

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Yeah, well hear this gnome-brain! (He slaps Kikazaru)

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: Ow! There was this recipe right.

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Stop it!

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: (Oof!) He liked it so much he married the cook! YEAH? Have some of this! (poke!)

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: AARGH not in the eye! (kick)

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: (Oof!) The King thought it was magic and AAAIEEE! He got a priest to curse us to keep quiet. (headbutt!)

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: Ha! Serves you right! Anyway, The priest only had enough ingredients for one curse, so when he tried to keep us all from telling anyone the recipe, each of us only got part of the curse. That's why I can't hear

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: anything now.

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: mmmmghhh (he nods his head)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me what this recipe is then?

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: How do you know about that - It's top secret information you know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I am also on a top secret mission.

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Ah I see, can you tell me what the mission is?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, it's on a strict need to know basis. I'm sure you understand.

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Of course, of course.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: However, I think I can trust you with sensitive material.

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (Iwazaru raises an eyebrow dubiously. You give him a wink.)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: (You whisper in Mizaru's ear) I've been charged with organising a secret party to celebrate the king's birthday, keep it quiet though, don't let a single monkey know.

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Well, in that case, you definitely do need to know the recipe.

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: The King has a real soft spot for Roasted Giant Snake stuffed with sliced banana and monkey nut paste.

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: GGHHMM! (Iwazaru starts jumping up and down)

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Yup, you need to find some bananas, and some monkey nuts and stuff a Giant snake with them, It's as simple as that. Stop hassling me Iwazaru.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I find this stuff then?

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Every monkey knows where to get hold of nuts and bananas, but I believe that the snakes live on a nearby island. Never seen one myself.

sections.title: Talking to the monkeys again without bananas or nuts

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I looking for again?

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Some monkey nuts, a banana and a snake to stuff them into.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I find this stuff then?

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: I've heard that there are snakes on a small island just to the east of here. Nuts and bananas? Just check out the normal places.

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: gghmnmmn !!!

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Stop making such a fuss Iwazaru.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure he's not trying to say something important....

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: No, no, no. Always looking to be the centre of attention that one. Don't let him worry you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...If you say so...

sections.title: Standing near the walls of the dungeon
Picking from it as a human
Using rope on it as a human
Using rope on it as a non-gorilla monkey
Talking to the wise monkeys without an amulet

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: Ook Ah Ook! Ah! Ook! Ook Ah Ook!

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Ook. Ah Ah! Ah Uh Ah! Ook. Ook.

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Ah Ah! Ook. Ook Ah Ook! Ook Ah Uh!

sections.title: Talking to the wise monkeys with a snake

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is this the right kind of snake, Iwazaru?

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: Iwazaru looks at the snake, sniffs it and nods sagely.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

sections.title: Entering/exiting the cave to cook the snake
Standing on the rockets without being a zombie monkey
Attempting to cook the snake unstuffed
Showing the wise monkeys the wrong ingredients

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Ah, monkey nuts. Good work

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (Iwazaru is hysterically trying to get your attention)

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Stop playing around Iwazaru.

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (Iwazaru grabs you by the arm)

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (He shakes his head vigourously)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think Iwazaru is trying to say something.

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Oh ignore him.

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: mmmh!! (He quickly makes some signs at Kikazaru)

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: Ah, I see...

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: Iwazaru says that you have the wrong kind of monkey nuts. Ordinary Monkeys nuts just won't do.

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: There is a special type of monkey nut that grows on the island and has a subtle and distinctive flavour that is highly valued by all monkeys with a sophisticated palate.

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: They are called Tchiki monkey nuts and require a very particular combination of soil and climate.

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: They need the minerals and dampness found in hill caves, but they also cannot grow without sunlight.

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: Myth and Legend shrouds the History of the Tchiki Monkey Nut.

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: Some say that it is the nut that gave monkeys their superior intelligence!

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: Others say Tchiki Monkey nuts are the food of the Monkey Gods.

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: Either way, you will have to find a path through treacherous undergrowth to harvest the Nut of Kings.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... He said all that?

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: Yup

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I never realised monkey sign language was so...concise. Anyway, where do I find this plant then?

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (Iwazaru nods enthusiastically and points South)

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: Look to the hills, south of here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right! I'd better start looking for this Tchiki Monkey Nut Bush then...

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: This feels like the banana, this should do the job.

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (Iwazaru Jumps up and down waving his arms and legs about wildly)

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Quiet! Iwazaru.

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (Iwazaru grabs you by the arm)

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (He shakes his head vigourously)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think your friend wants to tell me something.

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Oh ignore him, he's clueless.

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: GGGRRRMMHHGG!!

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (Iwazaru makes what you suspect was a very rude gesture to Mizaru)

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (Iwazaru starts gesturing at Kikazaru)

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: hmmm...

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (Iwazaru gestures some more)

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (Iwazaru waves his arms about yet more)

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: Oh that's very interesting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well....

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (Iwazaru sighs. And points to you.)

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: What, eh, oh right.

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: Iwazaru says that you have the wrong type of banana.

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: You need a red one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: (sigh), I should have guessed that it wouldn't be that easy. A red banana. Don't all bananas taste the same?

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (Iwazaru looks shocked)

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: (Mizaru looks astounded)

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: (Kikazaru looks blankly over your shoulder)

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: How can a monkey say such a thing!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, you mean RED bananas, yes, of course, I've heard that they are the tastiest. Much tastier than blue bananas. How could I forget. Bah, only a fake monkey wouldn't know that. Ahem.

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: Blue bananas? There's no such thing. Hey, wait a second where did you say you came from?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Errm I'm not from round here, anyway, moving quickly on, is there a red banana tree nearby?

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (Iwazaru nods enthusiastically and points West)

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Iwazaru says that there is a tree in the West of the Island.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right! I'd better start looking for a red banana tree.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where's this tchiki monkey nut bush again?

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Iwazaru says that there is a bush in a cave somewhere in the hills just south of the village.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

sections.title: Trying to enter the tchiki cave as gorilla or zombie monkey
Taking nuts after having them previously stolen by Mizaru
Showing the Tchiki monkey nuts or paste to the wise monkeys

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So these are definitely Tchiki Monkey nuts then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oi. I need these.

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: Iwazaru nods and gives you the thumbs up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where's this red banana tree again?

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Iwazaru says that there is a tree in the West of the Island.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I've got some Tchiki monkey nuts.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't eat them...

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (Iwazaru shrugs apologetically!)

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Mmm these are very tasty. Tchiki Monkey nuts really are the best nuts I've ever tasted.

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: But I'm afraid you're going to have to get some more.

sections.title: Using rope on tree with a full inventory
Taking banana after it was previously stolen by Miazure
Trying to take another red banana
Showing the red banana to the wise monkeys

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is this the red banana I'm looking for?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait! I need that, you don't have to eat...

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (Iwazaru slaps his forehead!)

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: Too late...

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Hmmm, oh yes very tasty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... is that the right one?

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Oh yes, I'd recognise the taste of red anywhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really... I suppose I'd better get another one now.

sections.title: Showing them a red banana again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is the right one?

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: Iwazaru nods and gives you the thumbs up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

sections.title: Talking to the wise monkeys without a stuffed snake after making one

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Have you cooked the snake yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I've lost it.

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: That was a bit careless wasn't it? You'd better go and make another one then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err what am I doing again?

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Get a red banana, some tchiki monkey nuts, and the snake corpse. Prepare the ingredients and stuff them into the snake. Simple. Even Kikazaru could do it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I cook this thing, it's huge! It's not going to fit in an oven or on a spit?

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: A very good question. Anyone know?

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (Iwazaru nods) He looks at Kikazaru to translate for him.

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: Zzzzzzzzzzzzzzz

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (Iwazaru prods Kikazaru)

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: Zzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzz

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (Iwazaru sighs, and then points to the floor directly beneath him, then acts like a zombie.

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: He then points at you and falls over and plays dead, gets up and then shrugs.)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm, somewhere underneath here, with zombies?

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (Iwazaru nods)

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (He then wipes his forehead as if it were hot and points at the sacrificial pyre in the east of the temple.)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Somewhere hot, nearby? What's this business about me dying though - that's not so cool.

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (Iwazaru shrugs)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I suppose I'd better have a look.

sections.title: Talking to the monkeys with a stuffed snake

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've stuffed the snake.

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: You'd better cook it now then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I cook this thing, it's huge! It's not going to fit in an oven or on a spit?

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: A very good question. Anyone know?

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (Iwazaru nods) He looks at Kikazaru to translate for him.

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: Zzzzzzzzzzzzzzz

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (Iwazaru prods Kikazaru)

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: Zzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzz

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (Iwazaru sighs, and then points to the floor directly beneath him, then acts like a zombie.

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: He then points at you and falls over and plays dead, gets up and then shrugs.)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm, somewhere underneath here, with zombies?

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (Iwazaru nods)

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (He then wipes his forehead as if it were hot and points at the sacrificial pyre in the east of the temple.)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Somewhere hot, nearby? What's this business about me dying though - that's not so cool.

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (Iwazaru shrugs)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I suppose I'd better have a look.

sections.title: Trying to cook odd stuffed snake
Trying to cook stuffed snake in a gnome cooker
Talking to wise monkeys after cooking without the cooked snake in inventory

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: You still here? You haven't lost the royal dish have you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Errr I'm afraid I have.

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: You'd better make another one then... You know what to do.

sections.title: Talking to wise monkeys with cooked snake

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Mmmm, something smells good...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yup, it's all cooked and ready to go, or at least I think it is.

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Maybe the King wouldn't notice if we had a little bit?

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: Just a little taste.

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: Iwazaru slaps both of the other monkeys on the back of the head!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, listen to Iwazaru - stay away from my stuffed snake! I'm not making another one. There was more than enough monkey business involved in making this one.

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Take that thing away from us. It will only fill us with unfulfilled longing...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for your help guys! I've had more fun than a barrel of monkeys doing this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Though I wish I'd realised earlier on that Iwazaru was the monkey with brains...

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Ooh. You cheeky monkey!

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: (Iwazaru mimes a crown on his head.)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Heh, time to serve this to the king...

sections.title: Talking without m'speak amulet to wise monkeys with cooked snake

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Ook Ah Uh! Ook Ah Uh! Ah Ah!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yup, it's all cooked and ready to go, or at least I think it is.

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Ook! Ah Uh Ah! Ook. Ook Ook! Ook.

lines.speaker: Kikazaru
lines.text: Ah Uh Ah! Ah Uh Ah! Ook Ah Uh!

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: Ook Ook! Ook Ah Ook! Ook! Ah! Ah!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, listen to Iwazaru - stay away from my stuffed snake! I'm not making another one. There was more than enough monkey business involved in making this one.

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Ook Ah Ook! Ah Uh Ah! Ook Ah Ook!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for your help guys! I've had more fun than a barrel of monkeys doing this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Though I wish I'd realised earlier on that Iwazaru was the monkey with brains...

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Ah Uh Ah! Ook Ah Ook! Ook. Ook.

lines.speaker: Iwazaru
lines.text: Ook Ah Uh! Ook Ah Ook! Ah Ah!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Heh, time to serve this to the king...

sections.title: Trying to feed King Awowogei without wearing m'speak amulet

lines.speaker: King Awowogei
lines.text: Err Where am I? Ooh! Roasted Stuffed Snake, lovely.

lines.speaker: King Awowogei
lines.text: Mmmm, I'm sure I can detect the delicate aroma of Tchiki Monkey nuts, and the sweet perfume of Red Bananas, all slowly caramelised on hot rocks.

lines.speaker: King Awowogei
lines.text: *munch* Who are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm just a wandering chef, travelling from town to town saving peoples lives.

lines.speaker: King Awowogei
lines.text: *gorge* You're a bit odd, aren't you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yet again, my heroic work goes unappreciated, I suppose that is the fate of a true hero.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So ... I've heard that this dish makes you more cooperative.

lines.speaker: King Awowogei
lines.text: *stuff* That's a lie, no definitely not. Well maybe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, getting to Ape Atoll is quite a pain. Do you have a way of making it easier to get there?

lines.speaker: King Awowogei
lines.text: N, Nnnnn..... nghghghnmnmn ... Yes there is a spell.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will you let me know how to cast it?

lines.speaker: King Awowogei
lines.text: Absolutely No... Nerrrrnnnn...Oh, go on then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, what do I need to know?

lines.speaker: King Awowogei
lines.text: Well, it will take a while to explain...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah I see, so the banana actually has the crucial effect on the teleportation matrix.

lines.speaker: King Awowogei
lines.text: Yes, yes, of course.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry I forced you to tell me the spell.

lines.speaker: King Awowogei
lines.text: Oh that's alright - it could have been worse - you could have asked me to marry you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes - that would have been a lot worse.

lines.speaker: King Awowogei
lines.text: Anyway, it seems you have done me a favour. Though I'm a bit confused about what went on here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're confused? Get a ticket and join the queue, Your Monkeyness.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Good work adventurer, I've teleported them away to safety! You only have [X] council member[s] left to protect!

sections.title: Trying to get more red bananas or tchiki monkey nuts
Talking to the wise old monkeys

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: Born from an egg on a mountain top,

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: The punkiest Monkey that ever popped,

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you singing?

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: It's an old Monkey Legend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like a fun story.

lines.speaker: Mizaru
lines.text: All monkey stories are fun. Maybe I'll tell it to you some time...
